docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Frameworm provides production-grade metrics for evaluating generative models.

docs.title: FID (Fréchet Inception Distance)
docs.text:
Measures quality and diversity of generated images.

Lower is better (0 = identical distributions) 

from frameworm.metrics import FID

fid = FID(device='cuda')
score = fid.compute(real_images, generated_images)
print(f"FID: {score:.2f}")

docs.title: Inception Score (IS)
docs.text:
Measures quality and diversity based on classifier confidence.

Higher is better (typical range: 1-10+) 

from frameworm.metrics import InceptionScore

inception_score = InceptionScore(device='cuda')
score, std = inception_score.compute(generated_images)
print(f"IS: {score:.2f} ± {std:.2f}")

docs.title: LPIPS (Learned Perceptual Similarity)
docs.text:
Measures perceptual similarity between images.

Lower is better (0 = identical, 1 = very different) 

from frameworm.metrics import LPIPS

lpips = LPIPS(device='cuda')
distance = lpips.compute(image1, image2)
print(f"LPIPS: {distance:.4f}")

docs.title: MetricEvaluator
docs.text:
Evaluate with multiple metrics at once: 

from frameworm.metrics import MetricEvaluator

evaluator = MetricEvaluator(
    metrics=['fid', 'is', 'lpips'],
    real_data=real_loader,
    device='cuda'
)

results = evaluator.evaluate(model, num_samples=10000)
# {'fid': 25.3, 'is': 8.5, 'is_std': 0.3, 'lpips': 0.45}

docs.title: Quick Evaluation
docs.text:
from frameworm.metrics import quick_evaluate

results = quick_evaluate(
    model,
    real_data=real_images,
    num_samples=5000,
    device='cuda'
)

docs.title: Automatic Evaluation
docs.text:
from frameworm.training import Trainer
from frameworm.metrics import MetricEvaluator

evaluator = MetricEvaluator(
    metrics=['fid', 'is'],
    real_data=real_loader,
    device='cuda'
)

trainer = Trainer(model, optimizer)
trainer.set_evaluator(evaluator, eval_every=5)

# Automatically evaluates every 5 epochs
trainer.train(train_loader, val_loader, epochs=100)

docs.title: Manual Evaluation
docs.text:
# Evaluate at specific points
results = evaluator.evaluate(model, num_samples=10000)

# Log to experiment
if trainer.experiment:
    for metric_name, value in results.items():
        trainer.experiment.log_metric(
            f"eval_{metric_name}",
            value,
            epoch=epoch
        )

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Use enough samples - At least 5000-10000 for FID/IS
  2. Match data distribution - Evaluate on same distribution as training
  3. Track over time - Monitor metrics during training
  4. Compare fairly - Same number of samples for all models
  5. Use multiple metrics - No single metric tells the whole story

docs.title: FID
docs.text:
• < 10: Excellent quality
  • 10-30: Good quality
  • 30-50: Moderate quality
  • > 50: Poor quality

docs.title: IS
docs.text:
• > 10: Excellent diversity and quality
  • 5-10: Good
  • < 5: Poor

docs.title: LPIPS
docs.text:
• < 0.1: Very similar
  • 0.1-0.3: Moderately similar
  • > 0.3: Quite different

docs.title: Out of Memory
docs.text:
# Reduce batch size
evaluator = MetricEvaluator(
    metrics=['fid'],
    real_data=real_loader,
    device='cuda',
    batch_size=50  # Reduce from default 100
)

docs.title: Slow Evaluation
docs.text:
# Use fewer samples for development
results = evaluator.evaluate(model, num_samples=1000)

# Use full samples for final evaluation
results = evaluator.evaluate(model, num_samples=50000)

docs.title: Model Zoo
docs.text: FRAMEWORM includes 6 production-ready generative models.

docs.title: VAE — Variational Autoencoder
docs.text:
Classic VAE with KL regularization. 

model = get_model('vae')(config)

Best for: Fast prototyping, smooth latent space

docs.title: DCGAN — Deep Convolutional GAN
docs.text:
Stable GAN training with spectral normalization. 

model = get_model('dcgan')(config)

Best for: Quick image generation

docs.title: DDPM — Denoising Diffusion
docs.text:
Original DDPM with linear noise schedule. 

model = get_model('ddpm')(config)

Best for: High quality unconditional generation

docs.title: VQ-VAE-2 — Vector Quantized VAE
docs.text:
Hierarchical codebook latents. Sharp reconstructions. 

model = get_model('vqvae2')(config)

Best for: Compression, tokenization, discrete representations

Key feature — two-level hierarchy: - Bottom level: local details (64x64) - Top level: global structure (16x16) 

z_top, z_bottom, vq_loss = model.encode(x)
recon = model.decode(z_top, z_bottom)

docs.title: ViT-GAN — Vision Transformer GAN
docs.text:
Transformer discriminator for global coherence. 

model = get_model('vitgan')(config)

Best for: High-resolution, globally coherent generation 

model:
  type: vitgan
  image_size: 64
  patch_size: 8
  vit_embed_dim: 384
  vit_depth: 6

docs.title: CFG-DDPM — Classifier-Free Guidance Diffusion
docs.text:
Conditional generation with guidance scale control. 

model = get_model('cfg_ddpm')(config)

Best for: Conditional generation, text-to-image, class-guided synthesis 

# Train conditionally
losses = model.compute_loss((x, class_labels))

# Sample with guidance
samples = model.sample(
    num_samples=4,
    class_label=3,          # Generate class 3
    guidance_scale=7.5       # Higher = more faithful but less diverse
)

Guidance scale guide: - 1.0 = no guidance (unconditional) - 3.0–5.0 = mild guidance - 7.5 = recommended (Stable Diffusion default) - 15+ = strong guidance, reduced diversity

docs.title: Comparison
docs.text: Model Quality Speed Controllable Memory VAE ⭐⭐ ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ ❌ Low DCGAN ⭐⭐⭐ ⭐⭐⭐⭐ ❌ Low DDPM ⭐⭐⭐⭐ ⭐⭐ ❌ Medium VQ-VAE-2 ⭐⭐⭐⭐ ⭐⭐⭐⭐ ❌ Medium ViT-GAN ⭐⭐⭐⭐ ⭐⭐⭐ ❌ High CFG-DDPM ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ ⭐ ✅ High

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Frameworm's plugin system allows you to extend the framework with custom components without modifying the core code.

docs.title: 1. Create a Plugin
docs.text:
Create a Python file in the plugins/ directory: 

# plugins/my_model.py
from frameworm.models import BaseModel
from frameworm.core import register_model

@register_model("my-model")
class MyModel(BaseModel):
    def __init__(self, config):
        super().__init__(config)
        # Your architecture here

    def forward(self, x):
        # Your forward pass here
        return x

docs.title: 2. Use Your Plugin
docs.text:
from frameworm.core import get_model, Config

# Auto-discovered!
model_class = get_model("my-model")
model = model_class(config)

That's it! No imports, no registration code in your main script.

docs.title: Models
docs.text:
@register_model("name", version="1.0", author="you")
class MyModel(BaseModel):
    def forward(self, x):
        ...

Requirements: - Inherit from BaseModel - Implement forward() method

docs.title: Trainers
docs.text:
@register_trainer("name")
class MyTrainer(BaseTrainer):
    def training_step(self, batch, batch_idx):
        ...

    def validation_step(self, batch, batch_idx):
        ...

Requirements: - Inherit from BaseTrainer - Implement training_step() and validation_step()

docs.title: Pipelines
docs.text:
@register_pipeline("name")
class MyPipeline(BasePipeline):
    def run(self, *args, **kwargs):
        ...

Requirements: - Inherit from BasePipeline - Implement run() method

docs.title: Datasets
docs.text:
@register_dataset("name")
class MyDataset:
    def __len__(self):
        ...

    def __getitem__(self, idx):
        ...

Requirements: - Implement __len__() and __getitem__()

docs.title: Auto-Discovery
docs.text: Plugins are automatically discovered when: - First access (get/list) - Manual discovery

docs.title: Disable Auto-Discovery
docs.text:
from frameworm.core import set_auto_discover

set_auto_discover(False)

# Now you must manually discover
from frameworm.core import discover_plugins
discover_plugins()

docs.title: Search
docs.text:
from frameworm.core import search_models

# Find all GAN models
gan_models = search_models("gan")

docs.title: Metadata
docs.text:
from frameworm.core import get_model_metadata

metadata = get_model_metadata("my-model")
print(f"Version: {metadata['version']}")
print(f"Author: {metadata['author']}")

docs.title: Registry Summary
docs.text:
from frameworm.core import print_registry_summary

print_registry_summary()

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. One plugin per file
  2. Descriptive names
  3. Add metadata (version, author, description)
  4. Include docstrings
  5. Test your plugins
  6. Keep dependencies minimal

docs.title: Organization
docs.text:
Organize plugins in subdirectories: plugins/ ├── models/ │ ├── gans/ │ │ ├── stylegan.py │ │ └── dcgan.py │ └── diffusion/ │ └── ddpm.py ├── trainers/ │ └── custom_trainer.py └── utils/ └── helpers.py

All .py files are discovered recursively.

docs.title: Unit Testing
docs.text:
# tests/plugins/test_my_plugin.py
from frameworm.core import register_model, get_model
from frameworm.models import BaseModel

def test_my_plugin():
    @register_model("test-plugin")
    class TestPlugin(BaseModel):
        def __init__(self, config):
            super().__init__(config)
        def forward(self, x):
            return x

    model_class = get_model("test-plugin")
    assert model_class is not None

docs.title: Integration Testing
docs.text:
def test_plugin_with_config():
    model_class = get_model("my-plugin")
    model = model_class(config)

    # Test functionality
    output = model(input_data)
    assert output.shape == expected_shape

docs.title: Common Issues
docs.text:
Plugin not discovered: 

from frameworm.core import reset_discovery, discover_plugins
reset_discovery()
discover_plugins(force=True)

Import errors: - Check plugin file has no syntax errors - Ensure all dependencies are installed - Verify plugin inherits from correct base class

Validation errors: - Ensure required methods are implemented - Check method signatures match base class

docs.title: Plugin Not Found
docs.text:
# Check if plugin was discovered
from frameworm.core import list_models, discover_plugins

print(list_models())  # See all models

# Force re-discovery
from frameworm.core import reset_discovery
reset_discovery()
discover_plugins(force=True)

docs.title: Import Errors
docs.text: Plugin files with import errors are skipped with a warning: Warning: Failed to import plugin plugins/my_model.py: ModuleNotFoundError Check your plugin file for errors.

docs.title: Validation Errors
docs.text:
If your plugin doesn't meet requirements: 

TypeError: Model class MyModel must implement forward() method

Ensure your plugin implements all required methods.

docs.text:
See examples/custom_plugin.py for complete working examples.

See plugins/README.md for quick reference.

docs.title: Simple Training Loop
docs.text:
from frameworm.training import Trainer
from frameworm.core import Config, get_model
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader

# Create model
model = get_model("vae")(config)

# Create optimizer
optimizer = torch.optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=0.001)

# Create trainer
trainer = Trainer(
    model=model,
    optimizer=optimizer,
    device='cuda'
)

# Train
trainer.train(train_loader, val_loader, epochs=100)

docs.title: Built-in Schedulers
docs.text:
from torch.optim.lr_scheduler import CosineAnnealingLR

scheduler = CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=100)
trainer.set_scheduler(scheduler)

from frameworm.training.schedulers import WarmupCosineScheduler

scheduler = WarmupCosineScheduler(
    optimizer,
    warmup_epochs=10,
    total_epochs=100,
    min_lr=1e-6

trainer.set_scheduler(scheduler)

trainer.add_callback(CSVLogger('training.csv'))

trainer.add_callback(MyCallback())

docs.title: Multiple Optimizers
docs.text: For models requiring multiple optimizers (like GANs), subclass Trainer and override train_epoch().

docs.text:
def custom_loss(outputs, targets):
    return ((outputs - targets) ** 2).mean()

trainer = Trainer(model, optimizer, criterion=custom_loss)

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Start with small learning rate - Use 1e-4 or 1e-3
  2. Use warmup - Especially for large models
  3. Monitor validation - Watch for overfitting
  4. Save checkpoints - Enable resume
  5. Use callbacks - Log everything
  6. Early stopping - Save time
  7. LR scheduling - Improve convergence

docs.title: Loss is NaN
docs.text:
• Reduce learning rate
  • Add gradient clipping (Day 8)
  • Check data normalization
  • Use mixed precision (Day 8)

docs.title: Not Converging
docs.text:
• Increase learning rate
  • Try different scheduler
  • Check model architecture
  • Verify data quality

docs.title: Out of Memory
docs.text:
• Reduce batch size
  • Use gradient accumulation (Day 8)
  • Enable mixed precision (Day 8)

docs.title: Encoder
docs.text:
• Input: (B, C, H, W) image
  • Conv layers to compress
  • Output: mean (μ) and log_variance (log σ²)

docs.title: Reparameterization
docs.text:
• Sample ε ~ N(0, 1)
  • z = μ + σ * ε

docs.title: Decoder
docs.text:
• Input: z (latent vector)
  • Transposed conv to reconstruct
  • Output: (B, C, H, W) reconstructed image

docs.title: Loss Function
docs.text:
• Reconstruction loss (MSE or BCE)
  • KL divergence: -0.5 * sum(1 + log(σ²) - μ² - σ²)

docs.title: Config
docs.text:
model:
  type: vae
  latent_dim: 128
  image_size: 64
  channels: 3
  beta: 1.0  # β-VAE coefficient
